data.narrative:
Ocius Technology is a Series B-stage defense & security company founded by Robert Dane. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 10 peers across the defense technology sector: 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms including Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Castelion, and Defense Unicorns.

The peer-median method anchors the primary valuation approach. Across the six most directly comparable same-sector, same-stage private peers, the median valuation is $1.2B, with a range spanning $1.0B to $1.5B. Named comparables include Vannevar Labs and Dream Security at $1.5B, Harmattan AI at $1.4B, and both Castelion and Defense Unicorns at $1.0B. This single methodological anchor reflects the limited availability of alternative quantitative approaches applicable at this stage and in this domain.

The triangulated valuation range is $1.0B (low) to $1.4B (high), with a median of $1.2B. This analysis is constrained by reliance on a single method and the inherent volatility in early-stage private company valuation. Key uncertainties include the execution risk associated with Ocius' A$176M Australian Program of Record contract awarded in March 2026 and the degree to which public defense primes' valuations should inform private frontier-tech comparables. Investors should weight peer medians against contract visibility and program maturity when establishing entry terms.
